name: Salt Rising Bread: Recipe/Tips
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and peel the potatoes well. Use Eastern potatoes, which are common in your area and cheapest.
2. Slice raw potatoes thinly into a large Pyrex or ceramic bowl (non-reactive).
3. Sprinkle cornmeal, sugar, salt over the potatoes, then pour boiling water over them.
4. Cover the bowl tightly with a heavy towel, then cover the towel with foil.
5. Place the covered bowl in a constant warm place overnight (such as on a radiator or low-heat warming tray set to 90°F). The foamy starter should develop within 12 to 15 hours. If it doesn't form foam or have a strong smell, discard and start over.
6. In a separate bowl, mix warm milk, baking soda, salt, melted shortening or oil, and warm water (even skim is fine).
7. Drain the potato mixture through a colander, saving the starter liquid to combine with the milk/water mixture.
8. Add enough flour (bread flour works best) to form a smooth dough. Knead for about 8 minutes, adding more flour as needed to prevent stickiness.
9. Divide the kneaded dough into 6 greased 1-pound loaf pans or 4 greased 9x3 loaf pans. Fill each pan about one-third full.
10. Place the covered pans in a consistent warm place (use a warming tray set to 90°F) until the breads are nearly doubled and risen to within an inch of the tops of the pans, about 1.5 to 2 hours.
11. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F for 30 minutes or until golden brown.
12. Brush tops with melted butter. The strong odor should not significantly affect flavor; instead, the bread has a pleasant taste and texture. Toasting enhances the flavor. This recipe freezes well.
